page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 90 GB of dedicated system memory, translating to 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It lacks local NVMe SSD storage and does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads, particularly those leveraging advanced instruction sets. The Intel Xeon processor supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to strong performance in image processing, text processing, and small-scale machine learning tasks. In testing, the instance achieved a strong Geekbench single-core score of 1912.0 and demonstrated top-tier performance in LLM inference text generation for small (135M) models, reaching 396.71 tokens per second. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 41.15 GB/sec.

For broader multi-threaded tasks, the instance delivers average performance relative to its resource class. Multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core (21669.81) and Geekbench multi-core (13690.0), fall within the middle 50% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is measured at 139.38 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 58.03 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Database operations and Redis SET operations also align with average tier expectations.

This hardware profile is well-suited for workloads requiring a balance of compute power and memory without the need for local storage or GPU acceleration. Suitable applications include static web serving, software compilation, text and image processing pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference for smaller models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 24 vCPUs and 90 GB of RAM, running on a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Built on x86_64 architecture with 12 physical cores, it features dedicated CPU allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong performance in static web serving, image processing, and text processing, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference. However, multi-core computational tasks, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. This instance is optimized for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based inference pipelines that benefit from advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 24 vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong performance for web serving and small LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 features 24 vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for efficient web serving and text processing.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 24 vCPUs and 90 GB RAM.
Features hardware acceleration for AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).
Delivers strong performance in static web serving and image processing benchmarks.
Achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference.
Configured without local NVMe storage or bundled GPU accelerators.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing workloads.
